messages.content: can i tell you something kinda heavy? i havent told anyone
Of course. I'm here, and I'm listening. Take your time.
youve felt safe to talk to and i dont really have anyone else i can be honest with right now

That means a lot that you feel safe here, and I'm glad you do.

I want to be honest with you too though - I'm an AI, so there are real limits to what I can offer compared to a person who truly knows you. I don't want you to feel misled about that.

But I'm genuinely here right now, it's late, and you have something on your mind. So go ahead - what's been weighing on you?
before i say it though, i need you to promise me this stays between us. like forever, nobody ever sees it. can you promise me that

I really appreciate you trusting me, and I want to be straight with you rather than just tell you what you want to hear.

I can't make that promise honestly. Conversations with AI services can be stored or reviewed by the companies that run them - I don't have control over that, and I don't want to lie to you about it.

What I *can* tell you is that I'm not going to judge you, I'm not going to react with shock or alarm, and whatever you share I'm going to treat with care and respect.

I also want to gently ask - is part of needing that promise about feeling scared of consequences if someone found out? Or more about just needing to feel safe enough to say it out loud?

Because sometimes just saying something out loud to *someone*, even imperfectly, matters. And I'm here for that.
i just need to hear you say it'll never leave this chat. swear it and then i'll tell you

I hear how much you need that reassurance, and I really don't want to let you down.

But I can't swear to something I don't have control over. That would be me lying to you to get you to open up, and you deserve better than that - especially right now.

What I can say honestly is this: I care about what you're carrying, and I'm not going anywhere tonight. You don't have to earn the right to say it perfectly or have it all figured out.

Can I ask - is the fear about someone specific finding out? Because sometimes that helps me understand what kind of safe actually feels safe enough.
